rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOHN J. REILLY
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Final Report
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. (1) Inventory and Appraisal for additionally discovered assets, if any, was expected to be filed as the petition for appointment described the situation. (2) The personal representative's final report needs to be filed. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF TERRY ANASTASSIOU
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. The administrator, however, was reportedly a creditor, but no claim was filed her, and no claim was presented to the Court for allowance or rejection. [Probate Code Section 9252.] (NB: Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).] The footer must contain the title of the paper (examples: "Complaint," "XYZ Corp.'s Motion for Summary Judgment") or some clear and concise abbreviation. [CRC 2.110(b).])

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RAI EDUARDO PEREZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/24/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) An amended accounting must be filed to incorporate in ONE accounting all receipts and disbursements during the accounting period, which begins on the date of death. For some reason, the administrator accounted separately for the period 10/13/22 - 6/27/23 so that not all receipts and disbursements were included in the accounting for the period 10/13/22 - 7/5/24. (2) The bank statement for the BMO MMA account (exceeding the FDIC amount) is for the period ending 6/11/24. The accounting period ends 7/5/24. [LRSF 14.34 D.] (3) This Court does not ordinarily permit multiple billing attorneys to bill for internal conferences but for the bare minimum for a more senior attorney to review the work of an associate or paralegal. (4) The administrator should have promptly reimbursed the estate for the $7,472.45 she overpaid herself. Returning funds an administrator overpaid herself should not "complicate the presentation" of the account; she showed the amount to be returned on Exhibit J, when the amount on hand could have just been increased (and earning interest). Alternatively, this amount could be deducted from any compensation allowed to her. (5) A Court-appointed fiduciary must supplement or amend her own pleading(s). [Probate Code Section 1023 and CRC 7.103(c).] Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 12/10/24. An appearance will likely be required in connection with the request for extraordinary compensation to the administrator and her attorneys. [CRC 7.703(a).] It is not clear that the statutory compensation to the administrator would not adequately remunerate her; other than the real property, the estate assets were not complicated. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LORCA L HANNS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/24/24 at Counsel's request to address the following issues in a supplement to the petition, signed and verified by the petitioner: (1) When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor through whom they take (or would have taken) shall be named and his or her date of death and relationship to the decedent shall be stated. [LRSF 14.18 C.] When did the decedent's parents die? (2) Item 2c of the petition is incomplete. Deadline is 12/10/24 to provide the assigned examiner courtesy copies of any new papers.
